script.narrative: Game resets to seed 42. All NPCs in spawn positions. Player is at (12, 7).
Player walks toward Research Lab (Emma's location). Takes ~3 seconds.
Camera centers on Research Lab. Emma is visible, idle at her spawn point.

expected_outcome.immediate: Emma enters TALKING mode. Thought bubble shows 'Listening...'. Chat panel shows cached first line: 'Tell me the outcome you want, and I'll restate it as constraints and acceptance criteria.'
expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: User sees Emma's response without network lag (cached).

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 1: Emma is responsive. Cached first line appears instantly.

expected_outcome.immediate: Emma enters THINKING mode. Thought bubble updates to 'Processing...'. HUD shows 1 active LLM request.
expected_outcome.delayed: After ~1.5s, Emma replies: 'Two weeks is your constraint. What are the acceptance criteria? What's locked vs optional?'
expected_outcome.memory_write: Fact extracted: 'user mentioned POC delivery in 2 weeks'
expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: User sees memory hook triggered (Emma remembers the timeline).

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 2: Real LLM dialogue, not canned. Emma responds to the specific message.
Wait for LLM response to arrive. Demo observer sees the 'Thinking...' state persist, then resolve.
Player walks to POC Factory (Olivia's location). Emma transitions back to IDLE and resumes waypoint walk.
Emma and Olivia are within the same street section. No interaction yet (distance > 1 tile).
Camera centers on POC Factory. Olivia is visible, transitioning between waypoints.

expected_outcome.immediate: Olivia enters TALKING mode. Thought bubble shows 'Listening...'. Chat panel shows cached first line: 'I'll translate goals into an execution plan and keep the run stable.'
expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: User sees Olivia's role is clearly different from Emma's.

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 3a: Three distinct NPC personalities in action. Olivia is the operator.

params.message: Emma just told me about the 2-week timeline. Can you break that into milestones?

expected_outcome.delayed: After ~1.5s, Olivia replies: 'Two weeks means we need week 1 delivery and week 2 validation. I'll sequence the work packages.'
expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: User realizes Olivia's reply references the 2-week constraint that Emma just mentioned.

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 3b: System memory is working. Olivia knows about the timeline.
Wait for Olivia's LLM response to arrive.
Player walks to Corner Café (Alex's location). Olivia transitions back to IDLE.

expected_outcome.interaction: Emma and Olivia may trigger an NPC-NPC scripted exchange if proximity ≤ 1 tile and cooldown expired. Example: Emma: 'Constraints are clear.' Olivia: 'Then I can sequence the work.'

script.narrative: OPTIONAL ACCEPTANCE MOMENT: NPC-NPC dialogue demonstrates system sophistication.
Camera centers on Corner Café. Alex is visible, idle at her spawn point.

expected_outcome.immediate: Alex enters TALKING mode. Thought bubble shows 'Listening...'. Chat panel shows cached first line: 'Welcome. Tell me your name once — I'll remember it during this run.'
expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: User sees Alex's role as the social interface.

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 4a: Alex is the human-facing interface. Already knows the system's constraints and actors.

params.message: My name is Jamie. Can you recap what Emma and Olivia said?

expected_outcome.delayed: After ~2s, Alex replies: 'Jamie, Emma locked the 2-week constraint. Olivia is breaking it into milestones. Here's what matters: delivery week 1, validation week 2. No scope creep without tradeoff.'
expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: User sees Alex remember the name AND synthesize the conversation.

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 4b: System memory persists across conversations. Alex references both Emma and Olivia by name and context.
Wait for Alex's LLM response to arrive.
HUD shows: 3 agents active, 0 current LLM requests. All NPCs in IDLE or WALKING state.
Player walks back to starting position. All NPCs resume autonomous movement and routine waypoint loops.
Game resets to same seed (42). All NPCs return to spawn positions exactly as before.

params.message: Replay identical run with same seed. NPC positions, timing, and dialogue should be byte-for-byte identical.

expected_outcome.acceptance_moment: Demo observer sees that RESET_RUN produces an identical initial state. Same seed = same game state = deterministic replay proof.

script.narrative: PROOF POINT 5 (Optional): Deterministic replay is foundational. Same seed, same street, same conversations (if player inputs identical).

Demo complete. Executive summary:
1. NPCs move autonomously (Emma, Olivia, Alex all walked routes).
2. Chat is real: specific replies to player messages, not canned.
3. Memory works: Alex knew Jamie's name, Olivia referenced the 2-week constraint.
4. NPC-NPC interaction possible (triggers demonstrated in script).
5. Determinism: RESET with same seed produces identical initial state.

Definition of Done: Executive understands the system in under 60 seconds by observing autonomy, chatting, and witnessing memory recall.

acceptance_criteria.autonomy_proof: All 3 NPCs visible moving through their waypoint routines without player input
acceptance_criteria.chat_proof: Player can click any NPC and receive a contextual reply within 2.5s
acceptance_criteria.memory_proof: At least one NPC references something the player said earlier in the demo (e.g., 'Jamie' or '2 weeks')
acceptance_criteria.npc_npc_proof: Two NPCs within 1 tile trigger a scripted exchange visible to the player
acceptance_criteria.determinism_proof: RESET_RUN(seed=42) produces identical initial state to first run; executive can verify by comparing NPC positions before any movement

definition_of_done: Executive watches 90-second demo, sees all 5 proof points, understands the system, can predict outcomes. No further explanation needed.
